heroHeadline: Day 34: Trump Claims Iran Begged for Ceasefire; Iran Denies — Presents 5-Condition War-End Proposal; Kharazi Wounded in Tehran Strike; IEA Warns April Oil Crunch 'Much Worse'; 4 Days to Hormuz Deadline
heroSubtitle: Trump publicly claimed Iran's president asked for a ceasefire on April 1 — Iran's FM Araghchi flatly denied it and instead presented 5 conditions for a permanent war end (not ceasefire): full halt to strikes, binding no-attack guarantees, reparations, ceasefire across all fronts, and Hormuz sovereignty recognition. A strike in Tehran wounded former FM Kharazi and killed his wife, disrupting the Pakistan diplomatic back-channel. Iran responded to Trump's 'destroyed military' statement with a new missile wave at Israel. Day 34 IDF strikes continued; IDF also hit Beirut killing a Hezbollah commander and 7 civilians. April 6 Hormuz deadline 4 days away — IEA warns the month will be 'much worse than March.'
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-02. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, The Intercept, The Hill, Axios, Pakistani Foreign Ministry.
